title: quercus sessiliflora var. coriacea
intro: Quercus sessiliflora var. coriacea, also known as the cork oak, is a variety of the Quercus sessiliflora tree, a species native to the mountainous regions of China. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits associated with Quercus sessiliflora var. coriacea include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. Herbal preparations of Quercus sessiliflora var. coriacea often include the bark, leaves, and roots, which can be made into teas, tinctures, or capsules for medicinal use.
